channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Recupera tu hábito de correr esta primavera
item.description: Si una temporada brutal de nieve y frío te ha mantenido en casa, estos consejos pueden ayudarte a retomar el paso.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman

media:content.media:credit: Landon Speers para The New York Times

item.title: How a ‘Dexter’ Star Is Singing Her Way Through Spanish Harlem
item.description: Luna Lauren Vélez, who played Capt. Maria LaGuerta on the serial killer drama, is making her own projects now (with much less blood).
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bahr

media:content.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times

item.title: Are New York’s Environmental Concerns Worsening a Housing Shortage?
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul is proposing to exempt a majority of new housing from state environmental reviews, arguing that sufficient safeguards are in place at the local level.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford

media:content.media:credit: Mimi d’Autremont for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul touring a residential construction project with Mayor Yadira Ramos-Herbert of New Rochelle.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 28, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Strike on Saudi Base Injures 12 U.S. Troops, Officials Say
item.description: The attack was one of the most serious breaches of American defenses since the war began on Feb. 28. Israel and Iran launched new strikes at each other on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times

item.title: El conflicto en Irán es un desafío para la diplomacia de Trump
item.description: Una mezcla de emisarios —un amigo, un familiar, un funcionario de línea dura y uno conciliador— en la crisis de Irán refleja el enfoque improvisado del presidente Trump.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A medida que la guerra se prolonga, el presidente Trump parece buscar una salida diplomática incluso cuando amenaza con una escalada.

item.title: Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach on Bringing ‘Dog Day Afternoon’ to Broadway
item.description: Jon Bernthal and Ebon Moss-Bachrach are both making their Broadway debut in a high-stakes adaptation of the beloved 1975 film “Dog Day Afternoon.”
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik

media:content.media:credit: Jonah Rosenberg for The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: March 28, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Saturday, March 28, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: An Irish Village Lifts Together To Free Children From Phones
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Saturday, March 28, 2026.

item.title: Trump Says Middle East Is ‘Saved’ as Iran Strikes Injure U.S. Troops in Gulf
item.description: At a Saudi event, the president said Iran was “begging to make a deal,” seemingly unaware of reports that an Iranian strike on a Saudi base had injured American troops.
item.dc:creator: Aishvarya Kavi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump spoke at a conference for Saudi Arabia’s sovereign investment fund even as news broke of an Iranian missile strike that injured U.S. troops.

item.title: ‘No Hard Feelings, Right?’
item.description: Don’t let Katie Hoody’s themeless puzzle make you hot under the collar.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Ben Stansall/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: This puzzle’s cooler than a poodle in the pool (but you’ll have to use your noodle).

item.title: Iranian Strike on U.S. Base in Saudi Arabia Injures 12 American Troops, Officials Say
item.description: The combined missile and drone attack amounted to one of the most serious breaches of American air defenses in the course of the monthlong war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron and Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Planet Labs, via Reuters
media:content.media:description: Satellite image shows planes at Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, last month.

item.title: Bank of America Pays $72.5 Million to Settle Lawsuit by Epstein Victims
item.description: The suit claimed the bank had overlooked signs that Mr. Epstein’s accounts were being used to further his abuse of young women.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Goldstein

media:content.media:credit: John Taggart for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A Bank of America spokesman said the bank “did not facilitate sex-trafficking crimes,” adding that “this resolution allows us to put this matter behind us and provides further closure for the plaintiffs.”

item.title: Clavicular, an Internet Narcissus, Is Arrested After Posting a Fight Video
item.description: The influencer, known for promoting handsomeness, is accused of arranging a brawl between two women. Separately, the authorities are investigating a video showing him shooting an alligator.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Cassidy Araiza for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The police in Florida arrested Braden Peters, known by his online persona Clavicular, accusing him of instigating a fight between two women and exploiting them by posting it online.

item.title: U.S. Antitank Mines Discovered in Neighborhood in Iran
item.description: Photos and video verified by The New York Times show mines dispensed by cluster bombs in a village a few miles from a missile site.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay and Aric Toler
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: We Analyzed the Deadly Crash at LaGuardia
item.description: Our graphics reporter Lazaro Gamio breaks down the second-by-second analysis leading up to the deadly plane crash at LaGuardia Airport.
item.dc:creator: Lazaro Gamio, Coleman Lowndes and James Surdam

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Friday
item.description: American officials sought to temper concerns of a possibly expanding military incursion after the Pentagon’s decision to deploy more troops to the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Eve Sampson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Diego Ibarra Sanchez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Israel on Friday launched airstrikes on Dahiya, an area on the outskirts of Lebanon’s capital that is a Hezbollah stronghold.

item.title: Judge Agrees to Drop Charges Against Officers in Breonna Taylor’s Death
item.description: The Trump administration had asked the court to dismiss the charges, describing them as an example of “weaponized federal overreach” by the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron

media:content.media:credit: Xavier Burrell for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial to Breonna Taylor at Jefferson Square Park in Louisville, Ky., in 2020.

item.title: Arrest Made in ‘Lovers Lane Murders,’ a 1990 Cold Case in Houston
item.description: The authorities charged a Lincoln, Neb., man with capital murder in the brutal killings of a young couple whose bodies were found in a wooded area.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

item.title: Man Accused of Killing Loyola University Chicago Student to Remain in Jail
item.description: Jose Medina, who is accused of being in the country illegally, is charged with murder in the death of Sheridan Gorman, a freshman at Loyola University Chicago.
item.dc:creator: Leigh Giangreco and Mitch Smith

media:content.media:credit: Jim Watson/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Karoline Leavitt, the White House press secretary, stood beside photos of Sheridan Gorman, a student who was shot and killed in Chicago.

item.title: How Long of a Wait at Security? For Many Passengers, It Was Anyone’s Guess.
item.description: As T.S.A. staff shortages continued on Friday, some airports saw interminably long wait times, while others barely had a wait at all, adding to travelers’ confusion.
item.dc:creator: Jacey Fortin, Shannon Sims and Michelle Baruchman

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: ICE agents checking in people before they go through security at LaGuardia Airport in Queens, New York.

item.title: When Will TSA Workers Be Paid? Here’s What We Know
item.description: President Trump on Friday directed the Homeland Security Department to pay thousands of airport security officers.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People waiting in line to go through security check at LaGuardia Airport in Queens, New York, on Friday.

item.title: N.Y. Attorney General Bars Health Provider After 3 Jail Deaths
item.description: The company, which Letitia James found was operating illegally in the state, also was fined $875,000 as part of a settlement.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Letitia James, the New York attorney general, found that inmates had been put “at serious risk” by the actions of a company.

item.title: Map: 4.1-Magnitude Earthquake Shakes Southern California
item.description: View the location of the quake’s epicenter and shake area.
item.dc:creator: William B. Davis, Madison Dong, Bea Malsky, Joey K. Lee, John Keefe and Judson Jones

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Esta ballena tuvo muchos ayudantes durante su parto. Mira el video
item.description: Este episodio protagonizado por un grupo de cachalotes se suma a las pruebas de que los humanos no son la única especie que recibe ayuda durante y después del parto.
item.dc:creator: Catrin Einhorn

item.title: Arrest Warrant for Ex-Kentucky Governor Matt Bevin Is Voided
item.description: The court decided the warrant was invalid, because it came after the former governor, Matt Bevin, had filed to disqualify a judge from his child-support case, accusing her of bias.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Luke Sharrett for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Matt Bevin, a former governor of Kentucky, had been sentenced to 60 days in county jail or turn over records.

item.title: Strikes on Iranian Industry Expand Blows to Civilian Economy
item.description: After Israel said it would intensify attacks on Iran’s industrial infrastructure, major steel plants and other vital plants were struck.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Shirin Hakim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Abedin Taherkenareh/EPA, via Shutterstock
media:content.media:description: Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan, Iran, in 2012. The governor of Isfahan said one person was killed and 15 were injured when Israel attacked the complex on Friday.

item.title: Hegseth Criticized for Removing Four Officers From Promotion List
item.description: Bitter rifts have opened over the defense secretary’s campaign to reverse policies that he says are prejudiced against white officers.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper, Greg Jaffe and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Members of the military during a speech by President Trump at Quantico, Va., last year.

item.title: For House Republicans, an Exodus Rivaled Only By Trump’s First Term
item.description: The number of Republicans departing the House this cycle is one of the highest since 1930.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Wu and Robert Jimison

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Trump Offers More Aid to Farmers, a Key Support Bloc Hurt by Tariffs and War
item.description: The president announced new loan guarantees at a White House event that appeared intended to reassure a group that has found itself caught in the crosshairs of some of his major policies.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green and Zolan Kanno-Youngs
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Pete Marovich for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A tractor signed by government officials at the White House on Friday. Among other things, President Trump said he was urging major tractor companies to “produce a bigger, better tractor at substantially less money.”

item.title: Robert Trivers, Eccentric Scientist Who Probed Human Nature, Dies at 83
item.description: A visionary evolutionary biologist, he drew comparisons to Charles Darwin with his theories on the genetic roots of seemingly detrimental behaviors like self-deception.
item.dc:creator: Michael S. Rosenwald

item.title: CPAC Considers Vance, Rubio and Life After Trump
item.description: The president’s potential successors “are mortal men,” one attendee said.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A pinto-bean poll about foreign intervention at the Conservative Political Action Conference.

item.title: Overlooked No More: Gertrude Chandler Warner, Author of ‘The Boxcar Children’
item.description: Her best-selling series, about four children who live in a train car and solve mysteries, inspired sequels, spinoffs and animated films.
item.dc:creator: Miguel Salazar

item.title: White House Vows to Pay T.S.A. Workers as Partial Shutdown Continues
item.description: Also, consumers are feeling the costs of the Iran war. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Ricardo Arduengo/Reuters

item.title: What Is Zillow Preview, and What Does It Mean for Home Buyers?
item.description: The massive real estate marketplace just changed its tune around pre-market listings, shifting the landscape for brokers and buyers.
item.dc:creator: Dorie Chevlen

media:content.media:credit: Carlos Bernate for The New York Times

item.title: Una actriz dice que descubrió al responsable de acosarla en línea: su esposo
item.description: Collien Fernandes dijo que su esposo había publicado imágenes sexuales ultrafalsas de ella. El relato, rebatido por su esposo, provocó indignación, protestas y debates parlamentarios en Alemania.
item.dc:creator: Christopher F. Schuetze

media:content.media:credit: Morris Macmatzen/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: La actriz Collien Fernandes habló el jueves en Hamburgo, Alemania, en una manifestación que protestaba contra los abusos a mujeres en internet.

item.title: Iran Moves to Assert Control Over Strait While Trading Strikes With Israel
item.description: Two ships turned back after being warned not to pass through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday. Iran said strikes in the country had hit a uranium processing plant and industrial sites.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward, Yeganeh Torbati, Adam Rasgon and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Cleaning up rubble in an apartment damaged by airstrikes in Tehran on Friday.

item.title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
item.description: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent for The New York Times

item.title: Why Young People Are Embracing Kennedy’s ‘Make America Healthy Again’ Lifestyle
item.description: Disillusioned with doctors, they went on a search for answers. They found supplements and a lot of red meat.
item.dc:creator: Coralie Kraft

media:content.media:credit: Elinor Carucci for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lexi Vrachalus spends about five hours every day creating content for her social media accounts.

item.title: Can Trump Claim Victory in Iran?
item.description: Is this really what winning the war with Iran looks like? Mark Dubowitz, the chief executive of the Foundation for Defense of Democracies, joins Ross Douthat on “Interesting Times” to make the case that Operation Epic Fury has degraded Iran’s military power enough to make victory achievable.

item.title: New York City Ballet Pulls Out of Kennedy Center Performances
item.description: The decision by the company, one of the most prestigious in the country, is the latest in a wave of high-profile cancellations at the center.
item.dc:creator: Adam Nagourney and Julia Jacobs
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Andrea Mohin/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A scene from New York City Ballet’s “Solitude,” choreographed by Alexei Ratmansky, which was to be performed at the Kennedy Center.

item.title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
item.description: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.
item.dc:creator: Richard Fausset
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “A ground invasion of Iran will make our country poorer and less safe,” Matt Gaetz, the former Florida congressman, warned the crowd at CPAC on Thursday.

item.title: Government Trolls Sling Memes in the Online Trenches of Mideast War
item.description: Officials in Tehran and Washington alike are trading taunts in English, often using American pop culture references.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Parin Behrooz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A billboard in Tehran this month showing Iran’s three supreme leaders since the Islamic Revolution.

item.title: Trump Demands the All-but-Doomed SAVE Act. The Value for Him May Be in the Fight.
item.description: The SAVE America Act, which the president wants to use to address issues like voting and transgender surgeries, allows him to try to shift the conversation from worries over inflation and war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump rattled off what he believes are the popular issues in the SAVE America Act at a Republican fund-raising dinner on Wednesday.

item.title: Everyone Thinks Parisians Rule Fashion. Everyone Is Wrong.
item.description: How the Antwerp Six shaped the modern fashion world, and why everyone is still obsessed.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman

item.title: Book Club: Let’s Talk About ‘Kin,’ by Tayari Jones
item.description: Jones’s new novel follows two motherless girls and their lifelong search for family.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times; inset cover: via Knopf

item.title: Ask a Journalist: Creating an Informative and Engaging Podcast
item.description: Wesley Morris, the host of the culture podcast “Cannonball,” joins us in April to chat with teenagers all about podcasting. Post your comments and questions for him by April 15.
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Kash Patel’s Emails Circulate Online as Iran Takes Responsibility for Release
item.description: What appeared to be personal emails from before Mr. Patel’s time as director were posted on a website that identified itself as Iranian but seemed to be hosted in Russia.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage, Adam Goldman, Aric Toler and Glenn Thrush
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Hacked emails and photos stolen from an old email account of Kash Patel, the director of the F.B.I., surfaced on the internet on Friday.

item.title: Fact-Checking the Debate Over the SAVE Act, the Republican Voting Bill
item.description: We examine claims justifying its restrictions, comparisons to current voting laws and warnings about its potential impact.
item.dc:creator: Linda Qiu

media:content.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A polling station at East End Community School in Portland, Maine, in November.

item.title: Kennedy Center Performer Asks Judge to Toss Case He Calls ‘Retaliatory’
item.description: A jazz drummer who called off a performance in protest of the center’s being renamed for President Trump said the president’s allies had sued him as punishment.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Kennedy Center in Washington was established as a living memorial to former President John F. Kennedy.
